That message is emitted from `handle_no_index` in `src/inject.rs:334`. It triggers when:

1. **No project DB exists** (no index has been created yet)
2. **More than 5 indexable files** are found
3. **Total LOC exceeds 5,000** — below that threshold it silently auto-bootstraps the daemon; above it, it emits the suggestion block to Claude Code asking whether to run `proactive-context init`

So you're hitting it because this project has 349 indexable files (~31k LOC) and no index exists yet.

that's a stupid design -- it should just start indexing in the background -- why wait or why ask cluade code or why show a bunch of files? -- if it doesn't have anything useful to say it shouldn't say anything
Builds clean. Now `handle_no_index` just silently starts the daemon whenever there are more than 5 indexable files — no file listing, no prompts, no noise.
